caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Owner-dentist compensation may be paid as distributions rather than W-2 wages, so the deterministic wage mix can understate owner clinical labor.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions are task-level judgments; no representative time-and-motion study was located for NAICS 621210.
inputs.pi_dist: The mix of FFS, PPO and Medicaid revenue is not measured for the deterministic target band; payer mix can materially change retention.
inputs.pi_moat: The source set supports practice structure and payer pressure, not a direct measured switching-cost estimate for a $1–10M EBITDA office.
inputs.t50_years: The 8.2% adoption observation comes from an ADHA/Heartland respondent survey rather than a representative census of US dental offices.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Survey respondents and their practices are not demonstrated to represent all US offices of dentists.
inputs.historical_analogs: No directly comparable historical dental-technology adoption series was located; this is a qualitative bridge rather than a calculated forecast.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This is not an observed NAICS 621210 owner-age statistic and is not segmented to the $1–10M EBITDA target band.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: The sources demonstrate transition and declining ownership rather than a quantified local shortage of buyers for every practice.
inputs.active_consolidators: No authoritative registry in the consulted source set counts active dental consolidators; the selected count is a broad estimate and the formula caps its effect.

inputs.buy_mult: S8 is an advisory-market compilation, not a transaction database; actual price varies sharply by specialty, owner-production dependence, geography and deal structure. The deterministic $1–10M target definition spans multiple S8 bands, while the sole class counted in n_band averages about $3.196M EBITDA.

inputs.exit_mult: This is a size-matched directional estimate, not evidence of an obtainable future exit multiple. A larger post-aggregation business could occupy a different size band, but no deterministic exit size is supplied, so the packet does not claim that uplift.
inputs.pricing_structure: No national target-band payer-mix split is used; the statement deliberately does not assign a single observed mix across this heterogeneous code.

dataset_inputs.n_band: The 369-firm estimate uses a 15.8% hospitals/healthcare-facilities margin proxy and employment-class averages; it is a floor rather than a census because some 20-99-employee dental groups may exceed $1 million of normalized EBITDA.

inputs.buy_mult / inputs.exit_mult: S8's 9x-11x band is for platform-grade regional groups; the deterministic size proxy establishes average EBITDA but not the platform attributes required for that band, so 10x remains directional.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1580 (margins.json[62]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Hospitals/Healthcare Facilities EBITDA/Sales 15.80% (closest named bucket for provider economics; public support-services bucket at 3.87% reflects a different business model)); revenue/employee $155,235 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 621210); class <5: 45,904 firms, avg revenue/firm $422,294 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $66,722 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 42,843 firms, avg revenue/firm $957,190 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $151,236 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 24,748 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,831,475 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $289,373 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 6,486 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,762,168 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $752,422 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 369 firms, avg revenue/firm $20,229,282 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,196,227 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 138 firms, avg revenue/firm $116,843,196 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $18,461,225 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 369

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: FDA and ADA evidence support assistive image and administrative uses, while physical clinical work remains clinician-led. Fractions represent task-level automation, not job elimination.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 621210 ($68,162,032,616) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 621210 ($160,195,607,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

The deterministic $1–10M target definition spans multiple S8 bands, while the sole class counted in n_band averages about $3.196M EBITDA.

source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact identity, schema, model route, frozen digests, deterministic dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. Reopened sources support the durable-demand and bounded administrative-automation thesis, and the corrected equal 10x entry and exit multiples remove any unsupported expansion from M. The remaining owner-age, succession, consolidator, payer-mix, adoption, target-count and valuation inputs are disclosed estimates or proxies; none changes the record's M=0, S=0 kill conclusion.

sources.title: What are the standards for AI use in dentistry?
sources.what_it_supports: ADA describes dental AI applications including image analysis and nonclinical claims, payment-integrity and quality-assurance areas, and the need for validation and responsible integration.

sources.title: FDA 510(k) summary K242600: Pearl Second Opinion PC
sources.what_it_supports: A current FDA-cleared dental image-analysis product aids radiograph review and detection and is explicitly not a replacement for a dentist's complete review or clinical judgment.

sources.title: AI in Dentistry: 2024 Heartland-ADHA survey
sources.what_it_supports: Survey respondents reported 8.2% of their practices already used dental AI and 50.2% were not at all prepared for adoption; it is directional evidence on current adoption and readiness.

sources.title: ADA responds to HHS request for information on AI adoption in dentistry
sources.what_it_supports: ADA says adoption remains uneven, particularly in small and mid-sized practices, and identifies data, interoperability, privacy and regulatory barriers.

sources.title: ADA HPI dental practice research
sources.what_it_supports: ADA HPI reports 72.5% private-practice ownership as of 2023, 16% dentist DSO affiliation as of 2024, and a continuing shift toward larger practices and DSOs.

sources.title: Dental Industry Predictions for 2025
sources.what_it_supports: ADA HPI's chief economist describes a generational transition and about one percentage point per year moving from solo toward group practice, while discussing private-equity affiliated practices.

sources.title: Dear ADA: Reimbursement rates
sources.what_it_supports: ADA reports that reimbursement rates are not keeping pace with inflation and practice expenses and discusses payer contracts, fee schedules, write-offs and negotiation.

sources.title: Dental Practice Valuation & EBITDA Multiples
sources.what_it_supports: A current advisory-market compilation describes size-stratified adjusted-EBITDA ranges: 5–7x for $500K–$1M, 7–9x for $1M–$3M, 9–11x for $3M–$5M+ and higher platform bands; it is directional rather than a transaction database.

sources.title: Medicaid Fee Schedules
sources.what_it_supports: ADA compiles state-specific dental Medicaid fee-for-service schedules and warns that schedules are state-specific, regularly updated and may omit payment streams.

title: Offices of Dentists
